edges.description: Without assembled axial/lateral elements, homologous chromosomes cannot synapse during meiotic prophase I.
edges.source: Synaptonemal complex axial/lateral element assembly failure

edges.description: Unsynapsed chromosomes trigger the meiotic-prophase checkpoint, arresting germ cells and inducing apoptosis.
edges.target: Pachytene-stage meiotic arrest and germ-cell apoptosis

edges.description: Germ-cell depletion from prophase arrest produces maturation arrest of spermatogenesis and non-obstructive azoospermia.
edges.source: Pachytene-stage meiotic arrest and germ-cell apoptosis

edges.target: Synaptonemal complex axial/lateral element assembly failure

nodes.description: With axial/lateral elements absent, homologous chromosomes fail to synapse at meiotic prophase I. Loss of SYCP3 also perturbs the chromosomal distribution of DNA repair/recombination proteins and of the transverse-filament protein SYCP1, leaving meiotic chromosomes unsynapsed.

evidence.reference_title: The murine SCP3 gene is required for synaptonemal complex assembly, chromosome synapsis, and male fertility.
evidence.snippet: While the absence of SCP3 affected the nuclear distribution of DNA repair and recombination proteins (Rad51 and RPA), as well as synaptonemal complex protein 1 (SCP1), a residual chromatin organization remained in the mutant meiotic cells.

nodes.description: In 46,XY individuals the meiotic-arrest pathway produces maturation arrest of spermatogenesis and non-obstructive azoospermia, the defining clinical presentation of SYCP3-related spermatogenic failure.

evidence.reference_title: Azoospermia in patients heterozygous for a mutation in SYCP3.
evidence.snippet: We identified in two patients a 1 bp deletion (643delA) that results in a premature stop codon and truncation of the C-terminal, coiled-coil-forming region of the SYCP3 protein.

nodes.description: Spermatocytes that fail to synapse arrest at meiotic prophase and are eliminated by apoptosis, depleting the germ-cell pool. In the mouse null, males are sterile because of massive apoptotic germ-cell death during meiotic prophase.
nodes.id: Pachytene-stage meiotic arrest and germ-cell apoptosis

evidence.reference_title: The murine SCP3 gene is required for synaptonemal complex assembly, chromosome synapsis, and male fertility.
evidence.snippet: homozygous mutant males were sterile due to massive apoptotic cell death during meiotic prophase.

evidence.reference_title: Azoospermia in patients heterozygous for a mutation in SYCP3.
evidence.snippet: We identified in two patients a 1 bp deletion (643delA) that results in a premature stop codon and truncation of the C-terminal, coiled-coil-forming region of the SYCP3 protein.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations of the SYCP3 gene in women with recurrent pregnancy loss.
evidence.snippet: Combined with the fact that similar mutations have been previously identified in two males with azoospermia, our current data suggest that sexual dimorphism in response to meiotic disruption occurs even in humans.

nodes.description: SYCP3 is a structural component of the axial/lateral element of the synaptonemal complex. Loss of functional SYCP3 — through a null allele in mice or a dominant-negative truncating variant in humans — prevents assembly of the axial/lateral elements and of the synaptonemal complex itself, so the scaffold required for homologous-chromosome synapsis fails to form during meiotic prophase I.
nodes.id: Synaptonemal complex axial/lateral element assembly failure

evidence.reference_title: The murine SCP3 gene is required for synaptonemal complex assembly, chromosome synapsis, and male fertility.
evidence.snippet: The SCP3-deficient male mice failed to form axial/lateral elements and SCs, and the chromosomes in the mutant spermatocytes

evidence.reference_title: Azoospermia in patients heterozygous for a mutation in SYCP3.
evidence.snippet: The mutant protein showed greatly reduced interaction with the wild-type protein in vitro and interfered with SYCP3 fibre formation in cultured cells.
